purpose: Portable migration log for recreating and auditing the ToolRegistry + chat-loop integration in another environment.

milestones.title: Introduce dynamic tool registry
milestones.summary: Added app.agent package and ToolRegistry to unify built-in and script-backed tools.
actions: Create app/agent package
Implement ToolSpec metadata model
Implement ScriptTool subprocess wrapper
Auto-discover tools/run/*.py as run_<script_name>
Expose execute() and describe() APIs
compatibility_notes: Registry supports both built-in Python callables and script tools.
Unknown tools now return a structured error containing available tool names.

milestones.title: Integrate registry into chat loop
milestones.summary: Replaced ad-hoc tool dispatch in app/chat.py with registry-backed execution and dynamic tool listing.
actions: Inject ToolRegistry into run_chat startup path
Use registry-driven instructions in system prompt
Route tool ACTION payloads through registry.execute
Keep bounded multi-tool loop (max 3 hops)
Keep PROCESS_PAUSE_SECONDS breathers between steps
compatibility_notes: Natural-language replies remain default; tool calls are opt-in via ACTION payload.
Tool inventory displayed by /tools comes from registry.describe().

milestones.title: Add quick-build intent
milestones.summary: Added direct command handling for build <name> [now] to scaffold projects without waiting for model tool formatting.
actions: Parse build command in interactive loop
Execute run_scaffold via registry
Log quick-build result to session log
compatibility_notes: Fast-path reduces UX failures from command-style prompts.
Falls back to normal model chat for non-build input.

milestones.title: Harden file tools for chunking and FAT32 limits
milestones.summary: Upgraded file helpers to chunked reads and 4GB-safe writes.
actions: Add _resolve for absolute/relative paths
Implement read_file(path, offset, chunk_bytes)
Implement write_file(path, content, append)
Enforce MAX_FILE_BYTES=4GB
Validate directory/file path types
compatibility_notes: Designed for removable media constraints (FAT32 class devices).
Chunk headers expose next_offset and eof for resumable processing.

milestones.title: Add script-tool metadata manifest
milestones.summary: Introduced tools/run/registry.json and consumed metadata in ToolRegistry for better tool selection and argument validation.
actions: Define script tool descriptions and arg signatures
Load manifest in ToolRegistry
Validate required args before executing tool
Enhance describe() output with signatures
compatibility_notes: Manifest missing/invalid -> registry gracefully falls back to defaults.
run_exec uses stdin for code payload compatibility.

milestones.title: CLI and operator docs
milestones.summary: Added manual discoverability and runtime operation docs.
actions: Add --manual flag
Document chat commands and tool flow
Document storage limits and troubleshooting
compatibility_notes: Documentation reflects registry-driven tool model and quick-build behavior.

milestones.title: Rebuild deterministic sovereign tool layer
milestones.summary: Replaced the tool layer with dict-compatible registry APIs, schema validation, side-effect enforcement, router execution, and legacy JSON shim compatibility.
actions: Implement dict-like registry accessors (__getitem__, get, __contains__, keys)
Define full per-tool schemas with required/type/default metadata
Add strict validation for required fields, type correctness, and unknown arguments
Auto-discover tools/run/*.py with subprocess wrapping, timeout, stdin support, and structured output
Enforce side-effect restrictions via read/write/exec/network policy map
Add deterministic model prompt block formatting sorted by tool name
Add route_tool_call() for safe tool existence checks, validation, policy enforcement, and execution
Preserve legacy execute(tool_name, tool_args) by mapping list args to schema names and returning stringified JSON
compatibility_notes: Maintains legacy llama.cpp tool loop compatibility while enabling structured router calls.
Structured return format is deterministic and JSON-friendly for both modern and compatibility paths.

milestones.title: Live hardware validation and defensive hardening
milestones.summary: First live USB hardware session. Fixed 17 bugs across PRs #16–#32. Graduated 5-level smoke test passed. Added router-driven tool budget, search arg-swap heuristic, Windows cp1252 fixes, context management for 2048 ceiling, and expanded stop tokens.
actions: Fix exec side-effect restriction blocking run_bash (PR #19)
Fix stdin mapping for bash.py (PR #20)
Rewrite bash.py for Windows subprocess compatibility (PR #21)
Force UTF-8 encoding across all tool outputs for Windows cp1252 (PRs #22, #28)
Add context management: truncated reads, tool output compression, memory gating (PRs #23-25)
Add Hamilton fault-tolerant arg-swap for run_search (PR #26)
Add repeat penalty (1.3) and expanded stop tokens (PR #26)
Fix isfile heuristic for arg-swap — directories no longer fool it (PR #28)
Add router-driven per-turn tool budget classification (PRs #29-30)
Add budget-aware tool loop with break on exhaustion (PR #32)
Add stop tokens for identity-confusion runaway patterns (PR #31)
compatibility_notes: All changes are .env-configurable and backward-compatible with higher-tier deployments.
Defensive code (arg swaps, budget limits, stop tokens) is harmless at higher model tiers.
J_TOOL_BUDGET env var defaults to 3; router classification overrides per-prompt.
LLAMA_REPEAT_PENALTY defaults to 1.3; reduce to 1.1 for larger models.

milestones.title: Endurance testing and router maturation
milestones.summary: Three rounds of 20-turn endurance testing drove router maturation. v1 (5/20 FAIL) → v2 (12/20 CONDITIONAL) → v3 (17.5/20 PASS). Phase 1 gate cleared. Router expanded: Windows shell commands, all registered tools (not just run_*), budget=0 answer acceptance, router breadcrumbs for J context recall, stdin-tools single-arg bypass, backslash→forward slash normalisation. System prompt enhanced with project facts, identity anchor, anti-Chinese directive.
actions: Add Windows shell commands (dir, del, type, copy, move, md, rd, cls, ver) to router
Replace run_* regex with dynamic all-tool first-word matching
Default list_dir to . when no args
Drop 20-char length check for budget=0 answers
Inject one-line router breadcrumbs into J message history
Add PROJECT FACTS block to J-system.txt (Python, Windows, Five Masters = AST governance)
Strengthen identity: Say I am J when asked, you are NOT Qwen
Add anti-Chinese directive to system prompt
Fix run_bash/run_exec arg splitting: pass entire rest as single stdin arg
Normalise backslashes to forward slashes in _split_args for Windows paths

milestones.title: Personality layer
milestones.summary: Scripted personality module (524 lines, 35+ functions). All terminal output sounds like J. Zero inference cost.

milestones.title: Multi-step execution: complete fix
milestones.summary: Five interlocking systems for reliable 25-call pipelines: dedup guard, breaker scaling, regex-rescue parser, phase digest, tool narration. 14 commits.

milestones.title: Phase-compression continuity anchor for serial reads
milestones.summary: Added explicit unread-file checklist tracking for prompts that ask to read each Python file in a directory, then reinjected the unresolved checklist in phase summaries to keep long tool runs on-task.
actions: Detect 'read each .py file in <dir>' prompts in _run_turn
Enumerate directory Python files into pending_read_targets
Remove files from pending list after run_read calls
Append unresolved checklist to phase compression summary prompt

milestones.title: GGUF host/local runtime toggle command
milestones.summary: Added interactive slash commands to switch llama.cpp model path between shard default and a host GGUF path without editing configuration files.
actions: Add /gguf status command
Add /gguf host to set LLAMA_MODEL_PATH to host gemma.gguf path
Add /gguf local to clear overrides and restore default path
Restart local llama.cpp server after toggle so path change takes effect

handoff_checklist: Ensure tools/run scripts exist and are executable by Python.
Ensure tools/run/registry.json matches available run_ tool names.
Verify /tools output lists both built-ins and run_* tools.
Verify 'build starter_agent now' scaffolds folder and logs output.
Verify file writes over 4GB are rejected with explicit error.
Verify route_tool_call() returns structured {ok, output/error} for known and unknown tools.
Verify side-effect restrictions block exec/network tools when restricted.
Verify setup.bat downloads Python, llama.cpp, model, and creates .env on a clean machine.
